pulse: The energy sector is currently trading at a median EV/EBITDA of 10.4x and a P/E of 18.4x, reflecting a slight premium over broader market valuations. Due to recent events such as the conflict in Iran, ExxonMobil (XOM) is seeing its stock pressured with an elevated EV/EBITDA of 11.4x and a trailing P/E of 22.5x, which translates to a higher risk profile amidst surging oil prices—recent reports indicated oil prices have surpassed $80. Moving forward, investors should remain cautious, as soaring oil prices could further compress margins across the sector, particularly impacting companies like Chevron (CVX), which is grappling with a significant decline in revenue growth at -8.2%.
